scope.target: Portable, fail-closed execution of the sanctioned v0.5.0 pre-tag critical gate
constraints: Keep the critical command allowlist exact
Resolve Git Bash on Windows without selecting the WSL launcher
Provide Git usr/bin only to the child process without mutating the parent environment
Decode subprocess bytes deterministically without reader-thread failures
Return deterministic failure details even when streams are empty
Keep v0.4.2 as an exact automatic upgrade source
Do not create or publish a tag
non_goals: Change release compatibility or migration semantics

steps: Resolve the active Windows Git installation to its Git Bash executable
Prepend the selected Git installation's usr/bin to the child environment
Merge and prove the real pre-tag command on main
Close lifecycle records on a continuation branch
validation: hosted package, governance, and Ubuntu gates
execution.last_completed_step: The repaired pre-tag runner completed the unrestricted critical gate at 958da6e: 33/33 passed, 0 failed, 0 deferred, and all 56 .NET tests passed. Nine focused portability tests also pass.
execution.next_action: Commit local-gate evidence, rerun the complete gate on the final clean candidate commit, validate the workflow commit range, then push for hosted verification.

results.summary: The bounded shell resolver, child environment, byte decoder, and combined-stream diagnostics are implemented; unrestricted local, hosted, and merged-main evidence remains pending.
tests_run: live Windows shell resolution: C:\Program Files\Git\bin\bash.exe
sandbox critical attempt: shell and decoding passed; three dotnet test restores failed with NU1301 network denial
unrestricted pre-tag runner critical gate at 958da6e: 33/33 passed, 0 failed, 0 deferred, 56 dotnet tests passed
results.residual_risk: Until the final candidate, hosted gates, and merged-main pre-tag pass, v0.5.0 is not release-ready.
